name: BBM RSI Mean-Reversion Snap
edge_hypothesis: Bollinger Middle Band (BBM_20_2.0 = SMA_20) is the statistical mean of price over the last 20 bars. In ranging markets — where ALL 8 ACCEPTs profit — price oscillates around this mean. When price crosses BACK through BBM from below, it signals the mean-reversion snap: price was extended downward and is now reverting to equilibrium. RSI < 45 confirms the move came from a weak position (not already overbought), ensuring we enter during the reversion phase rather than chasing an extended bounce. BBM has NEVER been tested as a signal in any strategy. This is fundamentally different from bollinger_breakout (which tests BBU/BBL breaks and is DEAD with 0 trades). The hypothesis: BBM crossing IS the canonical ranging signal because the middle band literally represents the range's center of gravity. If ranging markets dominate our alpha (PF 1.12-4.87 across all ACCEPTs), a signal that explicitly targets the range equilibrium should produce consistent edge.

variants.name: bbm_rsi_snap_v1_8to1
variants.description: Core thesis: BBM cross + RSI weakness filter. 8:1 R:R at 1.5 ATR stop. Tests whether BBM mean-reversion generates enough signal frequency on 4h while capturing consistent ranging alpha.

components.notes: Bollinger Middle Band (SMA_20). First ever BBM test. Statistical mean of price — the equilibrium level in ranging markets.
RSI weakness/strength filter. < 45 for longs ensures entry during reversion phase, not overbought extension.
ATR-based position sizing and R:R framework.

variants.name: bbm_rsi_snap_v2_tighter_rsi
variants.description: Tighter RSI gate (< 40 long, > 60 short). Tests whether deeper weakness before the BBM cross produces higher quality entries at the cost of signal frequency. If PF rises vs v1: deeper pullbacks before mean-reversion are higher conviction. If PF falls: the extra selectivity removes good trades.

components.notes: Tighter RSI gate (40/60). Tests deeper weakness before snap = higher conviction.

variants.name: bbm_rsi_snap_v3_tight_stop
variants.description: Champion-style tight stop geometry: 1.0 ATR stop, 10 ATR TP. The Vortex v3a champion proved that tighter stops improve PF because losers get cut faster while winners exit via natural reversal/TP. Tests the same tight-stop principle on a mean-reversion signal.

components.notes: Tight 1.0 ATR stop, 10 ATR TP — champion geometry.

indicator_roles.baseline: BBM_20_2.0 — Bollinger Middle Band (SMA_20). Statistical mean of price. Range equilibrium level. First system test.
indicator_roles.confirmation: RSI_14 — Weakness/strength directional filter. Ensures entry during reversion phase.
indicator_roles.volume_volatility: ATR_14 — Position sizing and R:R framework.
